page: Amazon Web Services c5n.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance within the c5n family, powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 72 vCPUs and 36 physical cores utilizing dedicated Intel Xeon 8124M processors running at 3.4 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, which translates to 2.67 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 66660.83 and a PassMark CPU score of 49891.05, both placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is also highly competitive, reaching 3704.68 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 208.32 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform in the top tier, with Redis achieving 61.1 million operations per second. However, single-core performance and text processing benchmarks sit in the average tier, and LLM text generation is weak, with the medium 7B model generating only 7.23 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5n.18xlarge offers high resource density for compute and network-heavy workloads without the added cost of local NVMe storage or GPU hardware. The baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps makes it highly efficient for distributed applications. Tradeoffs include the lack of local storage, which requires reliance on EBS, and its lower suitability for generative AI text generation. This instance is best suited for network-intensive compute workloads, database operations, multi-threaded decompression, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c5n.18xlarge is a compute-optimized, network-optimized instance featuring 72 vCPUs and 36 physical Intel Xeon 8124M cores running at 3.4 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database throughput, as evidenced by its Redis SET performance of 61.1 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation speeds are weak. With a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, this instance is highly cost-efficient for network-heavy, distributed compute tasks, though users must factor in the tradeoff of relying entirely on external EBS storage. It is well-aligned with workloads such as database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5n.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 72 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5n.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 72 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 100 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: A high-bandwidth, compute-dense platform designed for network-heavy workloads, multi-threaded processing, and distributed database operations without local storage overhead.
bullet_points: 72 vCPUs and 192 GB DDR4 memory powered by Intel Xeon 8124M processors.
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for high-throughput distributed tasks.
Strong multi-core CPU and database performance, including high Redis throughput.
EBS-only storage configuration with no local SSDs or GPU accelerators.
Suited for database operations, multi-threaded decompression, and web serving.
